subject: Integrated Science
style: Uganda flag-inspired classroom infographic; exact SVG text overlay; no generated text inside raster image.
title: 3.4 Energy Resources in the Environment
labels: How to Write a Science Report
Group Work: Compiling the Term 1 Report
details: Presenting the summary to the class.
evidence_excerpts: Prior knowledge activation regarding Term 1 Science practicals (Skeleton models, electricity circuits, and energy resources). Recalls and identifies key experiments conducted during Term 1. Reviewing the Muscular-Skeletal model.; Recalling the Electricity and Magnetism circuit.; Remembering the Energy Resources (biogas/solar) project.; Identifying materials used.

Introduction to the formal structure of a summary report (Title, Aim, Apparatus, Procedure, Results, and Conclusion). Identifies the components of a standard scientific summary report. Title and Aim (What and Why).; Apparatus/Materials (The tools).; Procedure (The steps).; Results and Conclusion (What happened and What it means).

Teacher demonstration of how to summarize the 'Electricity and Magnetism' experiment using the formal structure. Describes the process of synthesizing experimental data into a concise summary. Selecting key details.; Using scientific vocabulary (conductor, insulator, circuit).; Summarizing the observation.
